response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack engineer with a long, varied career, but the profile fundamentally mismatches this role. There is no evidence of native-level Japanese proficiency (a hard requirement), no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience, and no background in L2/L3 networking, switch products, or security technologies like MACsec or 802.1X. His C/C++ exposure is limited to one early role at National Instruments; his core strengths are web, TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, none of which the role centers on.
strengths: Long, diverse engineering career spanning web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded work
Solid Linux experience and low-level systems exposure (async I/O, high-throughput DLang services)
Strong software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship
concerns: No indication of Japanese language ability — a mandatory requirement for the role
No Linux kernel analysis/modification, networking-protocol, or security-technology (MACsec, 802.1X) experience
C proficiency for UNIX development is minimal and dated; career is centered on web/full-stack work

key_factors.factor: Broad senior engineering experience with Linux and systems programming
